---
title: "Buoyant Weight"
author: "Jill Ashey"
date: "2026-05-11"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(tidyverse)
library(seacarb)
```

Read in BW data 
```{r}
bw_data <- read.csv("../data/Physiology/buoyant_weight_experiment.csv", header = T) %>%
  mutate(Weight_g = as.numeric(bw_data$Weight_g)) %>%
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date, format = "%m/%d/%y"))
```

Extract and calculate final plug weights 
```{r}
avg_plug_weight <- bw_data %>%
  filter(Sample == "Final plug") %>%
  summarise(mean_wt = mean(Weight_g, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(mean_wt)

# Extract specific final plug weights
final_plugs <- bw_data %>%
  filter(Sample == "Final plug") %>%
  group_by(SampleID) %>%
  summarise(Plug_Weight = mean(Weight_g, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
```

Calculate all the things 
```{r}
coral_calculations <- bw_data %>%
  # Keep only coral measurements taken from 5/10/26 onwards
  filter(Sample == "Coral" & Date >= as.Date("2026-05-10")) %>%
  
  # Join plug weights and fill missing ones with the average
  left_join(final_plugs, by = "SampleID") %>%
  mutate(Plug_Weight = ifelse(is.na(Plug_Weight), avg_plug_weight, Plug_Weight)) %>%
  
  # Step 3: Net Buoyant Weight of the Coral
  mutate(Buoyant_Weight_Coral = Weight_g - Plug_Weight) %>%
  
  # Step 4: Temperature-dependent seawater density (converting kg/m3 to g/cm3)
  mutate(rho_sw = rho(S = 35, T = Temperature_C, P = 0) / 1000) %>%
  
  # Step 5: True Skeletal Mass (Jokiel Equation)
  mutate(Skeletal_Mass_g = Buoyant_Weight_Coral / (1 - (rho_sw / 2.93)))
```

Compute percent growth 
```{r}
baselines <- coral_calculations %>%
  filter(Date == as.Date("2026-05-10")) %>%
  select(SampleID, Baseline_Mass = Skeletal_Mass_g)

# Compute final percent changes
final_coral_data <- coral_calculations %>%
  left_join(baselines, by = "SampleID") %>%
  # Exclude any corals that were not measured on the May 10th baseline start
  filter(!is.na(Baseline_Mass)) %>% 
  mutate(Percent_Growth = (Skeletal_Mass_g - Baseline_Mass) / Baseline_Mass * 100) %>%
  select(Date, SampleID, Temperature_C, Weight_g, 
         Buoyant_Weight_Coral, rho_sw, Skeletal_Mass_g, Percent_Growth)
```

Join w/ metadata
```{r}
# Read and clean metadata
meta <- read.csv("../data/sample_manifest.csv", header = T)

# Final Join
final_coral_data_meta <- final_coral_data %>%
  left_join(meta, by = "SampleID") %>%
  na.omit()
```

Plot
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Ensure Date is recognized as a Date object if it lost its class during merging
final_coral_data_meta$Date <- as.Date(final_coral_data_meta$Date)

ggplot(final_coral_data_meta, aes(x = Date, y = Skeletal_Mass_g, color = Phenotype, group = SampleID)) +
  # Draw trajectories for individual coral fragments
  geom_line(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.6) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8, size = 1.8) +
  
  # Facet grid by Species (rows) and Treatment (columns)
  facet_grid(Species ~ Treatment) +
  
  # Clean, publication-ready styling
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(
    title = "Coral Skeletal Growth Over Time",
    subtitle = "Percent growth relative to 2026-05-10 baseline",
    x = "Sampling Date",
    y = "Percent Skeletal Growth (%)",
    color = "Phenotype"
  ) +
  
  # Clean up X-axis date formatting
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%m/%d", date_breaks = "4 days") +
  
  # Custom aesthetics for a polished look
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray30"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "italic", size = 11), # Italicizes species names beautifully
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
```

Another plot style
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Ensure Date is a factor or character to create discrete groupings on the X-axis like the example
final_coral_data_meta_plot <- final_coral_data_meta %>%
  mutate(Date_Label = format(Date, "%Y%m%d")) # Formats date exactly like '20260510'

ggplot(final_coral_data_meta_plot, aes(x = Date_Label, y = Skeletal_Mass_g, fill = Phenotype)) +
  # 1. Add the boxplots (outliers hidden because geom_jitter will display all points)
  geom_boxplot(aes(color = Phenotype), alpha = 0.6, outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.6, size = 0.8) +
  
  # 2. Overlay jittered raw points matching the boxplot dodging
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Phenotype), alpha = 0.5, size = 2,
              position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.15, dodge.width = 0.6)) +
  
  # 3. Match the exact 2x2 grid structure (Rows = Species, Columns = Treatment)
  facet_grid(Species ~ Treatment) +
  
  # 4. Apply the custom color scheme (Pink/Teal) for both fill and outline
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Resistant" = "#4b294fff", "Susceptible" = "#f3a17dff")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Resistant" = "#4b294fff", "Susceptible" = "#f3a17dff")) +
  
  # 5. Matching the labels and classic gray panel borders
  labs(
    title = "Skeletal Growth by Species, Phenotype, Treatment, and Timepoint",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Skeletal growth (BW)",
    fill = "Phenotype"
  ) +
  
  # 6. Styling aesthetics to match the clean background and bold text elements
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "plain", size = 16, margin = margin(b = 15)),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 13),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
    
    # Stylizing the facet banners
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "#D3D3D3", color = "black", size = 0.8),
    strip.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    strip.text.y = element_text(face = "bold.italic", size = 12), # Italics for species names
    
    # Legend placement at the bottom
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
    
    # Grid adjustments
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.8)
  ) +
  # Removes the duplicate color legend guide so only the fill legend shows
  guides(color = "none")
```
